extraction.method: blind — guides only, no spec access

claims.quote: Both expose two surfaces: a **public**, unauthenticated catalog you read to render storefront pages, and a **company**, token-authenticated surface you use to manage every row regardless of whether it is live.
claims.claim: Categories and collections each expose two surfaces: a public unauthenticated catalog and a company token-authenticated surface that manages every row regardless of live state.

claims.quote: Public catalog reads resolve the company from the request subdomain
claims.claim: Public catalog reads resolve the company from the request subdomain.

claims.quote: They take no credentials.
claims.claim: Public catalog reads take no credentials.

claims.quote: Company operations require a Bearer token and are called against `https://api.fluid.app`.
claims.claim: Company operations require a Bearer token.

claims.quote: Read operations need the `storefront.view` scope; writes need `storefront.update`.
claims.claim: Company read operations require the storefront.view scope.

claims.quote: Read operations need the `storefront.view` scope; writes need `storefront.update`.
claims.claim: Company write operations require the storefront.update scope.

claims.quote: Legacy per-resource grants (such as `categories.update`) are still accepted during the migration.
claims.claim: Legacy per-resource scope grants such as categories.update are still accepted during the migration.

claims.claim: Draft, scheduled, archived, and inactive rows never appear in the public category catalog.

claims.quote: <ParamField query="q" type="string">
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a q query parameter of type string.

claims.quote: Free-text search over the translation-aware `title` and `description` fields.
claims.claim: The q parameter performs free-text search over translation-aware title and description fields.

claims.quote: <ParamField query="filter[country]" type="string">
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a filter[country] query parameter of type string.

claims.quote: ISO-2 country code. Restricts results to categories available in that country.
claims.claim: filter[country] takes an ISO-2 country code and restricts results to categories available in that country.

claims.quote: Unrestricted categories (those with no country list) match every country.
claims.claim: Categories with no country list are unrestricted and match every country in filter[country].

claims.quote: <ParamField query="filter[language]" type="string">
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a filter[language] query parameter of type string.

claims.quote: ISO locale. Restricts results to categories translated in that locale.
claims.claim: filter[language] takes an ISO locale and restricts results to categories translated in that locale.

claims.quote: <ParamField query="filter[source_type]" type="string">
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a filter[source_type] query parameter of type string.

claims.quote: One of `product`, `medium`, `page`, `library`, or `promotion`. Categories only.
claims.claim: filter[source_type] accepts exactly the values product, medium, page, library, promotion.

claims.quote: One of `product`, `medium`, `page`, `library`, or `promotion`. Categories only.
claims.claim: filter[source_type] applies to categories only.

claims.quote: <ParamField query="filter[parent_id]" type="string">
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a filter[parent_id] query parameter of type string.

claims.quote: A category id (as a string), or `"root"` for top-level categories only. Categories only.
claims.claim: filter[parent_id] takes a category id as a string, or the literal "root" for top-level categories only, and applies to categories only.

claims.quote: <ParamField query="sort" type="string">
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a sort query parameter of type string.

claims.quote: One sort key: `position`, `title`, or `created_at`.
claims.claim: The sort parameter accepts the keys position, title, and created_at.

claims.quote: Prefix with `-` for descending (for example `-created_at`).
claims.claim: A sort key can be prefixed with - for descending order.

claims.quote: Defaults to `position` then `id`.
claims.claim: The default sort is position then id.

claims.quote: <ParamField query="lang" type="string">
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a lang query parameter of type string.

claims.quote: Response locale as an ISO code (for example `fr`). Omit to use the company default.
claims.claim: lang sets the response locale as an ISO code; omitting it uses the company default.

claims.quote: On the public surface, any field missing in the requested locale falls back to `en`.
claims.claim: On the public surface, any field missing in the requested locale falls back to en.

claims.quote: Paging is cursor-based. `meta.pagination.next_cursor` and `prev_cursor` carry the cursor for the adjacent page; pass it back as `page[cursor]`.
claims.claim: Paging is cursor-based: next_cursor/prev_cursor carry the adjacent page cursor, passed back as page[cursor].

claims.quote: pass it back as `page[cursor]`.
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a page[cursor] query parameter.

claims.claim: A page[limit] larger than the maximum returns 422.

claims.quote: The go-live is applied at read time: once `publish_at` has passed, a stored `scheduled` row resolves to `published` in API responses automatically — you don't make a second call to flip it.
claims.claim: Go-live is applied at read time: once publish_at has passed a stored scheduled row resolves to published in API responses automatically, with no second call.

claims.quote: Combined with `active: true`, that makes the row live exactly at `publish_at`.

claims.quote: A scheduled category does not appear in the public catalog or resolve on its public slug until `publish_at` passes.
claims.claim: A scheduled category does not appear in the public catalog or resolve on its public slug until publish_at passes.

claims.quote: A scheduled collection is not in the public catalog either, but it does resolve on its public slug beforehand (as `noindex`), which is useful for previewing a pre-launch page.

claims.quote: curl "https://acme.fluid.app/api/v202604/categories?filter[country]=GB"
claims.claim: GET /api/v202604/categories exists (public category catalog).

claims.quote: `filter[country]` uses "OR-empty" semantics: it returns rows explicitly available in that country **plus** every unrestricted row (rows with an empty country list), since an unrestricted row is available in that country too.
claims.claim: filter[country] uses OR-empty semantics: it returns rows explicitly available in that country plus every unrestricted row.

claims.claim: Country availability is written via country_isos and queried via filter[country].

claims.quote: `country` is a product-only parameter that selects country-relative pricing, and it does not filter category or collection availability.
claims.claim: country is a product-only parameter that selects country-relative pricing and does not filter category or collection availability.

claims.quote: Collections handle country availability exactly like categories: read `countries`, write `country_isos` (empty array clears, omitted key leaves unchanged), and filter with `filter[country]`.
claims.claim: Collections handle country availability exactly like categories: read countries, write country_isos (empty clears, omitted leaves unchanged), filter with filter[country].

claims.quote: Categories can be nested into a tree: a category may have a parent and any number of children.
claims.claim: Categories can be nested into a tree: a category may have a parent and any number of children.

claims.quote: Hierarchy is a **category-only** feature. Collections have no parent/child relationship
claims.claim: Hierarchy is a category-only feature; collections have no parent/child relationship.

claims.quote: their responses carry no `parent_id`, `has_children`, or `position`, and their write bodies accept no `parent_id`.
claims.claim: Collection responses carry no parent_id field.

claims.quote: their responses carry no `parent_id`, `has_children`, or `position`, and their write bodies accept no `parent_id`.

claims.quote: their responses carry no `parent_id`, `has_children`, or `position`, and their write bodies accept no `parent_id`.

claims.quote: their write bodies accept no `parent_id`.
claims.claim: The create-collection body accepts no parent_id field.

claims.quote: <ResponseField name="parent_id" type="integer | null">
claims.claim: Category rows carry a parent_id field of type integer or null.

claims.quote: The id of the parent category, or `null` when the category is at the root (top level).
claims.claim: parent_id is the id of the parent category, or null when the category is at the root.

claims.quote: <ResponseField name="has_children" type="boolean">
claims.claim: Category rows carry a has_children field of type boolean.

claims.quote: `true` when the category has at least one child. Use it to decide whether to fetch a level deeper.
claims.claim: has_children is true when the category has at least one child.

claims.quote: <ResponseField name="position" type="integer">
claims.claim: Category rows carry a position field of type integer.

claims.quote: The category's order among its siblings. The default sort (`position` then `id`) uses it.
claims.claim: position is the category's order among its siblings and the default sort (position then id) uses it.

claims.quote: To nest a category under another, set `parent_id` to the parent's id when you create it. Leaving `parent_id` out (or `null`) creates a top-level category.
claims.claim: Setting parent_id to the parent's id at create nests a category; leaving it out or null creates a top-level category.

claims.quote: set `parent_id` to the parent's id when you create it.
claims.claim: The create-category body accepts a category.parent_id field.

claims.quote: curl -X POST "https://api.fluid.app/api/v202604/company/categories" \
claims.claim: POST /api/v202604/company/categories exists (create category).

claims.quote: "parent_id": 4821,
claims.claim: Create-category request body nesting a child via title, parent_id, and position.

claims.quote: "position": 1
claims.claim: The create-category body accepts a category.position field.

claims.quote: You can also move an existing category by patching its `parent_id`. Set it to another id to re-parent, or to `null` to promote it to the top level:
claims.claim: An existing category is moved by patching parent_id: another id re-parents it, null promotes it to the top level.

claims.quote: by patching its `parent_id`.
claims.claim: The update-category body accepts a category.parent_id field.

claims.claim: PATCH /api/v202604/company/categories/{id} exists (update category).

claims.quote: "parent_id": null
claims.claim: Update-category request body promoting a category to the top level by setting parent_id null.

claims.quote: Use `position` to order siblings within a level.
claims.claim: position is used to order siblings within a level.

claims.quote: The public catalog's `filter[parent_id]` selects a single level of the tree. Pass the literal string `"root"` to get only top-level categories:
claims.claim: filter[parent_id] selects a single level of the tree; the literal string "root" returns only top-level categories.

claims.quote: The public catalog's `filter[parent_id]` selects a single level of the tree.
claims.claim: The public category catalog accepts a filter[parent_id] query parameter.

claims.quote: curl "https://acme.fluid.app/api/v202604/categories?filter[parent_id]=root"
claims.claim: GET /api/v202604/categories exists (public category catalog).

claims.quote: Pass a category id (as a string) to `filter[parent_id]` to get that category's direct children:
claims.claim: Passing a category id (as a string) to filter[parent_id] returns that category's direct children.

claims.quote: Each returned child carries its own `has_children`, so you know whether it has a further level beneath it.
claims.claim: Each returned child carries its own has_children indicating whether it has a further level beneath it.

claims.quote: Combine `filter[parent_id]` with `has_children` to walk down the tree one level at a time:
claims.claim: Combining filter[parent_id] with has_children walks down the tree one level at a time.

claims.quote: For each returned category where `has_children` is `true`, fetch its children with `filter[parent_id]=<id>`.
claims.claim: For each returned category where has_children is true, fetch its children with filter[parent_id]=<id>.

claims.quote: A category with `has_children: false` is a leaf.
claims.claim: A category with has_children false is a leaf.

claims.quote: To walk **up** the tree instead, read a category's `parent_id` and fetch that parent by id with the company show endpoint `GET /api/v202604/company/categories/{id}`.
claims.claim: To walk up the tree, read a category's parent_id and fetch that parent by id with the company show endpoint.

claims.quote: fetch that parent by id with the company show endpoint `GET /api/v202604/company/categories/{id}`.
claims.claim: GET /api/v202604/company/categories/{id} exists (company category show).

claims.quote: The public show endpoint resolves by slug rather than id, so on the public surface you locate the parent among catalog results (each row carries both `id` and `slug`) and then fetch it with `GET /api/v202604/categories/{slug}`.
claims.claim: The public show endpoint resolves by slug not id, so on the public surface you locate the parent among catalog results and fetch it by slug.

claims.quote: then fetch it with `GET /api/v202604/categories/{slug}`.
claims.claim: GET /api/v202604/categories/{slug} exists (public category show).

claims.quote: each row carries both `id` and `slug`
claims.claim: Public catalog rows carry an id field.

claims.quote: each row carries both `id` and `slug`
claims.claim: Public catalog rows carry a slug field.

claims.quote: `filter[parent_id]` is available on the **public** catalog only, which returns live categories.
claims.claim: filter[parent_id] is available on the public catalog only, which returns live categories.

claims.quote: The company index (`GET /api/v202604/company/categories`) does not accept `filter[parent_id]`.
claims.claim: The company category index does not accept filter[parent_id].

claims.quote: a key the write schema doesn't define is rejected with `422`, not ignored
claims.claim: Write bodies are strict: unknown keys are rejected with 422 rather than silently ignored.

claims.quote: read-only fields like `countries` and `seo` are not writable keys
claims.claim: The category write schema has no countries field (reads use countries, writes use country_isos).

claims.quote: read-only fields like `countries` and `seo` are not writable keys
claims.claim: The category write schema has no seo field (SEO is written via search_engine_optimizer_attributes).

claims.quote: every response returns it under `seo`
claims.claim: The create response returns the SEO object under category.seo.

claims.quote: You write SEO as `search_engine_optimizer_attributes`, but every response returns it under `seo`.
claims.claim: SEO is written as search_engine_optimizer_attributes but read back as seo; round-tripping a response seo object into a PATCH does not apply it.

claims.quote: `401` returns a bare `{"message": ...}` object
claims.claim: A 401 response is a bare object carrying message.

claims.quote: `403` returns a bare `{"error": ...}` or `{"message": ...}`
claims.claim: A 403 response is a bare object carrying either error or message, depending on the failure.

claims.quote: only `404` and `422` use the wrapped error envelope
claims.claim: A 422 response uses the wrapped envelope with error.message.

claims.quote: only `404` and `422` use the wrapped error envelope
claims.claim: Only 404 and 422 responses use the wrapped error envelope; 401 and 403 are bare objects.

claims.quote: the public catalog and the category slug expose only live rows, while a collection slug also resolves draft, scheduled, and inactive collections
claims.claim: Public exposure differs by surface: catalog and category slug are live-only; a public collection slug also resolves draft, scheduled, and inactive rows.
